meta.title: Specialised Investment Funds — Research Dashboard
meta.source: Derived from SIFs peer-comparison; returns/performance sections intentionally excluded.

primerHtml:
In a move to bridge the gap between traditional mutual funds and Portfolio Management Services (PMS), SEBI introduced a new category — Specialised Investment Funds (SIFs) — effective 1 April 2025. Designed for seasoned investors, SIFs allow sophisticated strategies such as long-short positions, sector rotation and dynamic asset allocation, with a minimum investment of ₹10 lakh, regulated under SEBI's mutual fund framework.

Key Takeaways

  • The full form is Specialised Investment Fund — a new SEBI category bridging traditional mutual funds and PMS.
  • Offers sophisticated, strategy-based options sitting between mutual funds and PMS.
  • Minimum ₹10 lakh per investor (PAN level) across all SIF strategies within a single AMC — unless accredited.
  • Permits long-short strategies, sectoral rotation and dynamic asset allocation for greater flexibility.
  • Redemption may be less frequent; AMCs may impose notice periods of up to 15 days.
  • Best suited to experienced / high-net-worth investors comfortable with volatility who don't need high liquidity.

What is a Specialised Investment Fund?

A SIF is a new investment structure introduced by SEBI (effective 1 April 2025) that lets AMCs offer strategy-focused schemes with greater portfolio flexibility than traditional mutual funds.

  • Serves investors seeking advanced asset-allocation methods within a regulated framework.
  • Permitted to use complex strategies — long-short equity, sector positioning, active asset allocation — and can take unhedged short positions up to 25% of the portfolio using derivatives.
  • Suitable for investors comfortable with moderately complex products who can meet the ₹10 lakh minimum.
  • May follow equity-oriented, debt-oriented or hybrid strategies.

How Do SIFs Work?

SIFs operate under SEBI Mutual Fund Regulations as a distinct category of strategy-specific schemes, structured as open-ended or interval funds. AMCs can invest across listed equities, debt, commodity derivatives and REITs/InvITs, and may take unhedged shorts of up to 25% of net assets via derivatives — capturing both upward and downward moves.

Investors must meet the ₹10 lakh minimum (accredited investors exempt). SIP, SWP and STP are permitted as long as the overall commitment meets the threshold. Redemption can be less frequent (daily to monthly or fixed maturity), with notice periods up to 15 working days, plus strict disclosure norms.

SEBI Regulations & Minimum-Investment Rules (2025)

  • The ₹10 lakh minimum is mandated under Regulation 49X(1), per investor at PAN level across all SIF strategies of a single AMC.
  • The threshold is not scheme-specific — it is the total commitment across all SIFs under one AMC, excluding regular MF schemes.
  • Accredited investors are exempt from the ₹10 lakh minimum.
  • SIP / SWP / STP allowed provided the cumulative commitment meets the threshold.
  • Daily monitoring applies — passive market-driven breaches are fine; active redemptions below ₹10 lakh may require exit.

Strategies Allowed Under SIFs

A. Equity-Oriented Strategies

  • Equity Long-Short Fund — min 80% equity; up to 25% unhedged short; open-ended/interval; daily redemption.
  • Equity Ex-Top 100 Long-Short Fund — min 65% outside top 100 by market cap; up to 25% short in mid/small caps.
  • Sector Rotation Long-Short Fund — min 80% equity across a maximum of four sectors; up to 25% short at sector level.

B. Debt-Oriented Strategies

  • Debt Long-Short Fund — fixed-income across durations; shorts via exchange-traded debt derivatives; interval; weekly redemption.
  • Sectoral Debt Long-Short Fund — debt across ≥2 sectors (≤75% in one); up to 25% short within a sector.

C. Hybrid Strategies

  • Active Asset Allocator Long-Short Fund — equity, debt, derivatives, REITs/InvITs, commodity derivatives; max 25% short; interval; redemption twice a week or more.
  • Hybrid Long-Short Fund — min 25% equity, min 25% debt; up to 25% unhedged short across both; interval.

Benefits & Risks

✓ Differentiated strategiesLong-short equity, tactical allocation and sector rotation not found in regular MFs.
✓ Cross-asset diversificationEquities, debt, derivatives, REITs/InvITs and commodities in one wrapper.
✓ Customised exposureFocused, high-conviction portfolios run by experienced managers.
✓ Enhanced-return potentialShort selling & dynamic rebalancing aim for alpha even in falling markets.
✗ High ₹10 lakh minimumRestricts access to HNI / financially-prepared investors.
✗ Lower liquidityLess-frequent redemption, notice periods up to 15 days, possible exit loads.
✗ Market & manager riskEquity, rate & credit risk plus dependence on manager skill.

SIF vs Traditional Mutual Funds

FeatureSIFTraditional MF
Investor eligibilityAccredited, HNI & institutionalGeneral public incl. retail
Minimum investmentFrom ₹10 lakh (PAN level)As low as ₹100
RegulationSEBI MF Regs with special SIF provisionsSEBI Mutual Fund Regulations
LiquidityLower — daily to quarterly; lock-in / notice possibleHigh — daily redemption
Asset flexibilityNiche/alternative incl. derivatives, REITs, commoditiesPrimarily stocks & bonds
Risk profileHigh — short selling, concentration (0% leverage)Moderate to high by type

Who Should Invest?

  • Investors with surplus capital (₹10 lakh+) willing to commit for a longer horizon.
  • Those who understand derivatives, long-short positions and asset-allocation shifts.
  • Investors wanting non-traditional strategies unavailable in regular mutual funds.
  • Those comfortable with lower liquidity, market fluctuation and higher volatility.

categories.desc: SMID-cap long-short strategies investing in companies beyond the Top 100 by market cap (min 65% Ex-Top 100), with up to 25% unhedged short via derivatives. Both benchmark to Nifty 500 TRI.

sections.title: Investment Strategy & Core Allocation
sections.note: iSIF — Asset Allocation Framework  vs  qsif — Indicative Allocation, Risk & Ranges. Both maintain min equity (long+short); qsif min = 65%; 0% leverage.
rows: Ex-Top 100 (Mid/Small) incl. ≤25% unhedged short: 65%–100% — core SMID long + short alpha
Other Equity & related + Debt: 0%–35% — large caps, debt & MM, debt MF units

sections.title: Methodology & Investment Approach
rows: Fundamental Focused — stock selection driven by valuations & earnings revision; bottom-up research on the Ex-Top 100 universe.
Signal Generation (MARCOV + quantamine) — ingests macro, microstructure, sentiment, liquidity & volatility; MARCOV at 70% HFA + 30% LFA with liquidity/tradability/regime filters.
Technical Focused — market-trend signals time and size exposure to mid & small caps.
Stock Selection (Long & Short) — Long: mid & small caps; Short: mid caps in F&O basket; money-manager inputs overlay quant signals.
Macro Focused — liquidity and the Real Effective Exchange Rate (REER) guide allocation.
Portfolio Construction & Sizing — Risk Management Matrix + Money Management Matrix + Smart Order Routing.
Bottom-Up Selection — long high-conviction mid/small caps; short overvalued names via derivatives (≤25%).
Dynamic Rebalancing & Monitoring — daily monitoring, stress tests, small/mid rebalancing, attribution & model updates.

sections.title: Stock Selection Criteria
rows: Potential Market Leader — well-established player with strong network
Long Book — high-quality mid & small caps; strong balance sheets, earnings visibility, scalable models
Strengthening Balance Sheet — low leverage, cash rich, tangible assets
Short Book — mid caps in the F&O basket; overvalued or fundamentally weak names
Low Cost Producer — cost advantage, entry barriers, economic moat
Signal Engine — MARCOV (70% HFA + 30% LFA) → liquidity/tradability → regime filters
Earnings Potential — high earnings visibility & growth across cycles
Human Overlay — money-manager inputs finalise long & short selection
Positioning — high active share vs benchmark; opportunistic shorting

sections.title: Long-Short / Derivative Toolkit
rows: Arbitrage — short futures against the underlying stock
Covered Calls — short calls against the underlying stock
Covered Call / Protective Put — income & downside protection (low risk)
Portfolio Hedging — buy index puts or short index futures
Bear Put / Bear Call Spreads — directional spreads (low/moderate risk)
Protective Options — buy puts or long calls to protect positions
Long & Short Options/Futures — gain from declining or advancing prices
Short Call / Put / Straddle — high-risk option-writing strategies
Bear Put / Bear Call Spreads — buy & short options at different strikes
Unhedged (Naked) Shorts — up to 25%; a distinct alpha source
Stock & sector selection by market outlook

sections.title: Investment Framework & Philosophy
rows: Philosophy — aims for risk-adjusted returns with lower volatility
Systematic Active Investing — rules-based, conviction-driven, risk-aware; blends machines & money-manager insight
Universe — Ex-Top 100 focus; companies beyond the top 100, targeting broader-market opportunities
MARCOV — cross-asset, regime-aware; long winners, short laggards, curb drawdowns
Valuations — long attractive mid & small caps; short overvalued ones to manage beta
HFA (High Frequency Analytics) — real-time microstructure intelligence; 70% HFA + 30% LFA
quantamine Platform — integrated intelligence & execution across macro, microstructure, sentiment, liquidity & volatility

sections.title: Why This SIF — What Makes It Unique
rows: Deep Ex-Top 100 / SMID universe backed by ICICI Pru research depth (₹1,49,530 Cr Ex-Top 100 exposure across schemes).
India's 1st SMID-cap long-short fund — a genuinely new category combining small & mid cap with up to 25% short.
Long-short flexibility — up to 25% unhedged short to actively manage volatility, unavailable in long-only SMID funds.
Systematic Active Investing via MARCOV + HFA (70/30) — a quant, data-driven edge.
Bottom-up multi-factor selection (fundamental + technical + macro) for lower-volatility, risk-adjusted returns.
quant heritage: AUM grown from ~₹35 Cr (2020) to ~₹96,000+ Cr; deep derivatives & dynamic-management DNA.
Mutual-fund wrapper — transparency, daily NAV, equity taxation (LTCG 12.5%) at a ₹10 lakh entry.
0% leverage with up to 25% unhedged short; beta optimisation (month-end beta 0.49).

Reduction in overall portfolio volatility (month-end beta 0.49).
Up to 25% short reduces portfolio volatility vs long-only SMID funds.
Enhanced diversification and reduced drawdowns via the short book.
Backed by ICICI Prudential's research depth and Ex-Top 100 experience.
Additional source of alpha from opportunistic short positions.
MF structure: transparency, daily NAV, equity taxation (LTCG 12.5%).
Unleveraged structure (0% leverage) — a controlled risk profile.
Targets lower-volatility, risk-adjusted SMID returns over a 3-yr+ horizon.
India's 1st SMID long-short with a MARCOV + HFA systematic edge.

sections.title: Investment Process
rows: Bottom-up research on the Ex-Top 100 universe (Mid & Small caps).
Investment universe → quantamine data cleaning & processing.
Screen on fundamental (valuations, earnings revision), technical (market trend) and macro (liquidity, REER).
MARCOV signal generation (70% HFA + 30% LFA) → liquidity/tradability & regime filters → money-manager inputs.
Long high-conviction names; short overvalued ones via derivatives (≤25%).
Long & short stock selection → position sizing (Risk + Money Management Matrix) → portfolio construction.
Dynamic asset focus by scenario: Positive → SMID up to 100%; Negative → min 65% SMID, ≤35% large cap, more call-writing.
Smart Order Routing → risk & compliance → daily monitoring, stress tests → attribution & model updates.

sections.title: Conclusion
rows: A research-driven Ex-Top 100 long-short from India's largest AMC. Combines SMID-cap growth with up to 25% short to manage volatility, in a transparent, equity-taxed MF wrapper. Best for investors seeking risk-managed SMID exposure over 3-year+ horizons who value research depth and a fundamental bottom-up process.

India's first SMID-cap long-short, powered by a systematic MARCOV + HFA engine. With 0% leverage, up to 25% unhedged short and beta optimisation (0.49), it targets asymmetric, lower-drawdown returns. Best for investors wanting a quant-driven, actively risk-managed SMID alpha layer alongside long-only equity.

sections.title: How These Funds Are Unique / Different
sections.note: A factual, like-for-like read — the differentiation matrix.

sections.title: Are They Genuinely Different? — Plain-English Read

items.p: All six are SEBI SIF Equity Long-Short funds sharing an identical structural skeleton: minimum 80% equity, up to 25% unhedged short via derivatives, ₹10 lakh minimum, equity taxation, and the same “participate in upside, cushion the downside” pitch. Five of six benchmark to Nifty 500 TRI (only Diviniti uses Nifty 50). At the wrapper level they are near-identical — the regulation makes them so.

items.h: Where they genuinely DIFFER (the part that matters)
items.p: 1) Process DNA splits the field in two: fundamental/discretionary (Arudha = quality long + 4 engines; Diviniti = strategic-core + tactical; DynaSIF = qual+quant blend) vs systematic/quant (Sapphire = pure multi-factor; qsif = VLRT+MARCOV+HFA; Titanium = valuation-tool net-equity). 2) Track record: Diviniti and qsif are the only two with real live data. 3) Risk posture: Diviniti is the conservative outlier (net equity ~26.5%, heavily hedged); the rest run fuller directional/derivative exposure. 4) Cost & terms vary sharply: qsif ~11.34% and Sapphire ~4.81% (early annualised on tiny AUM — likely to normalise); exit windows range 15 days (qsif) to 1 year (Sapphire). 5) Covered calls are a headline engine only for Arudha.

The real fork is fundamental vs systematic. Live track record → Diviniti or qsif; conservative/hedged → Diviniti (most defensive posture); covered-call income engine → Arudha; pure rules-based factor model → Sapphire; AI/quant money-flow → qsif; large-franchise discretionary-quant blend → DynaSIF; valuation-driven dynamic net-equity from a large AMC → Titanium. Watch the headline Expense (Reg) ratios on qsif and Sapphire — distorted by very small launch AUM, they should fall as assets grow. The edge is in execution and team, which only a real track record will prove.

sections.title: Conclusion (per fund)
rows: Risk-managed equity built on four return engines, with covered-call income as a distinctive lever. Backtest pitches equity-like returns at lower vol. For sophisticated investors comfortable with derivative-driven L/S risk. (Track record yet to build.)

The most established of the set — live since Dec-25 with a real, currently defensive portfolio and a seasoned L/S CIO, and the most conservative posture of the set. For investors wanting a proven-team, downside-aware L/S vehicle anchored in large caps.

Dynamic, quant-blended, cap-agnostic L/S from a large equity franchise, with explicit alpha-over-benchmark mandate & active beta management. For investors wanting an unconstrained, regime-aware approach.
Valuation-tool-driven L/S from Tata AMC; dials net-equity (65–100%) across phases, hedges/shorts scaling in weak markets. Backed by a large AMC. Newest; track record yet to build.
Pure rules-based multi-factor quant L/S from Franklin Templeton, run by a factor specialist. Sector-agnostic, emotion-free, repeatable. For investors preferring systematic over discretionary.

Quant's systematic-active L/S via VLRT, MARCOV & HFA — flexi-cap, derivative-driven, predictive. Longest live record of Batch 2 with early positive returns. For investors comfortable with high-conviction systematic strategies.

categories.desc: Equity + debt blend with limited short via derivatives (interval structure). Seven funds. Benchmarks differ (CRISIL Hybrid 50+50 / 85+15 / NIFTY 50 Hybrid Composite Debt 50:50) — compare each fund to its own benchmark only.

categories.title: Sector Rotation Long-Short
categories.desc: A focused sectoral long-short strategy: min 80% equity (long + short) restricted to a maximum of four sectors, with up to 25% unhedged short at sector level. Currently a single live offering — qsif Sector Rotation Long-Short Fund (Quant MF). Benchmark: Nifty 500 TRI.

single.name: qsif Sector Rotation Long-Short Fund
facts: Sandeep Tandon · Sameer Kate · Jignesh Shah · Ankit Pande · Sanjeev Sharma
Systematic Active Investing — MARCOV + HFA · Cycles Analytics

alloc.title: Indicative Asset Allocation, Risk & Ranges
alloc.note: Minimum equity exposure (long + short) = 80%. Long-short applies at sector level — all stocks of a sector are either long or short. 0% leverage as per SEBI regulations.

model.title: Flexible 4-Sector Allocation Model
model.desc: At any time the fund takes exposure to a maximum of 4 sectors (focused, avoiding over-diversification). No same-sector long-short — a sector is either fully long or fully short.

dispersion.title: Why this fund — leadership rotates rapidly
dispersion.desc: Best vs worst quarterly sector return across 9 Nifty sectoral indices. The gap frequently exceeds 30% — no single sector dominates consistently. Source: quant Global Research.

advantages.d: Focused exposures give targeted sector insight without over-diversification.
Participate in complex sector rotation without needing deep market-timing skills.
Expert managers actively run the book for strong risk-adjusted performance.
Dynamically realigns long & short exposures to enhance structural resilience.
Adjustments executed without generating taxable events — full compounding.
Aligns exposures with economic trends — no sector selection needed by investors.

phases.title: Relative performance across market phases
phases.desc: How a qsif equity-centric (SIF) strategy is expected to behave vs a long-only quant equity fund. Indicative only.

compare.title: qsif vs quant MF vs AIF
compare.desc: How the SIF wrapper compares with quant's own mutual-fund schemes and AIFs (from the deck).

p: “Returns in equities are driven more by where you invest than what you pick.” The fund expresses a focused thesis on sectoral cycles rather than overall market direction — letting investors avoid sector-timing, while shorting sectors expected to face headwinds adds a distinct alpha source.

The engine: Systematic Active Investing via MARCOV + HFA, underpinned by quant's Cycles Analytics (macro, earnings and liquidity cycles) and a top-down filter — Global Macro → India Macro → Domestic Themes → Sector Selection → Stock Selection.

categories.title: Active Asset Allocator Long-Short SIF
categories.desc: Interval structure. Dynamic allocation across equity, debt and derivatives with up to 25% unhedged short. Three funds — DynaSIF, iSIF and qsif — each benchmarked to its own composite index.
